messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] BayesSpace maintainership
messages.body_plain:
We have given access and the package continues to fail in both release and
devel.
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.22/bioc-LATEST/
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.23/bioc-LATEST/

This is very problematic.  Please fix your package in the next few weeks to
avoid deprecation and removal of the package.  This will need to be
corrected on both the RELEASE_3_22 branch and devel branch on
git.bioconductor.org.  For further questions or assistance please reach out
on the Bioconductor developers mailing list  bioc-devel at r-project.org

We appreciate your quick attention to this matter and continued
contribution to Bioconductor.

Please Note:  You should be getting automatic notifications of failure from
BBS-noreply at bioconductor.org. If you are not, please check spam folders and
consider allowing notifications from this email address.

messages.body_plain:
Hi Lori ,

I am writing to follow up on your message below.
You mentioned you would be in touch off the mailing list to ask further questions and coordinate updates, but I have not yet heard back.

Could you please let me know if there are any updates or if you need anything further from my side?

Thank you in advance for your help.

Best regards,
Emanuela


Da: Kern, Lori <Lori.Shepherd at RoswellPark.org>
Data: marted?, 23 dicembre 2025 alle ore 13:49
A: Emanuela Iovino <emanuela.iovino at unibo.it>, bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>, bioc-devel-request at r-project.org <bioc-devel-request at r-project.org>
Oggetto: Re: uncoverappLib repository update
I apologize for the delay. I was away on holiday.  I can assist with this request.  I will be in touch off mailing list to ask some further questions and coordinate updates.

Cheers,



Lori Kern

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Buffalo, New York 14263

From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Emanuela Iovino <emanuela.iovino at unibo.it>
Sent: Saturday, December 13, 2025 4:07 PM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>; bioc-devel-request at r-project.org <bioc-devel-request at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] uncoverappLib repository update

Dear Bioconductor Team,

I am writing regarding the uncoverappLib package.

Due to an issue with my GitHub account (loss of access keys), I had to recreate the repository where the development version of uncoverappLib was hosted. As a consequence, the current GitHub repository history does not fully reflect the original development timeline.

In the meantime, I have made important updates to the package, including (v.2.1) (https://secure-web.cisco.com/1-U0OToUty0B-4e_W4mHNOpNkIPb4gMbY2HXiv05XnA9OhG8VclQ1zw28DN1fh5XzjagJQ9tk11pUQrPFOYeRxbKC6IRKtHgM_7S5u4bil_s8uNlCU7Ym9EaCa_VdJD-GDf3Xvx2dI6lU8s_zRXYbM7lgyC3QGwzfU_zXz6kjqtvWVN7MlRUVLacK9uY4mVUUjurC8RspLrARqf0sSwQR6nv9yu61Iw1IUg1nRpGrGyEKZKZjElkf3pZFrZYvSvVcvHA1MQLKtMhMi5HOn19T0yMLrh1C89EdcU6TT18GAwUJQMp5ko6V5vkrxFaXFgDF/https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Femanuela04%2FuncoverappLib%2Ftree%2Fv2.1.0):

  *   bug fixes in hg38
  *   improvements to existing functionality and the speed of library
  *   general maintenance updates

I would like to kindly ask for guidance on how to proceed with integrating these updates into the next Bioconductor release, and whether there are any recommended steps for handling the repository transition and package assembly under these circumstances.

Thank you very much for your time and support.

Best regards,
Emanuela Iovino, PhD

Computational Genomics Unit - BOSCO
IRCCS Azienda Ospedaliero-Universitaria di Bologna
Policlinico di Sant'Orsola, Pav. 1, Via Pelagio Palagi 9, 40138 Bologna

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Submission: PARATI v0.99.0
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor developers,

I would like to submit my R/Bioconductor package **PARATI** for review.

Package name: PARATI
Version: 0.99.0
GitHub repository: https://github.com/newche/PARATI
R version: ? 4.1
Bioconductor compatibility: tested with current release

**Description:**
PARATI infers maternal and paternal transmitted and non-transmitted alleles
from trio genotype data.
It enables SNP-specific analyses of genetic nurture and transgenerational
effects. The package supports homozygous trios, mixed
homozygote/heterozygote, and triple heterozygotes using haplotype
similarity with a user-defined window.

**Dependencies:**
- Imports: data.table, dplyr, stringr, optparse, openxlsx, vcfR, methods
- Suggests: knitr, rmarkdown, BiocStyle, testthat
- Optional: PLINK 1.9 (for exporting PLINK bed/bim/fam files)

**Vignette and Examples:**
- The package contains a vignette `parati-workflow.Rmd` demonstrating a
minimal example using the included testing dataset
(`Toy_TrioGenotype.vcf.gz` and `Toy_FamilyIndexTable.xlsx`).

**Testing:**
- R CMD check completed successfully without errors.
- All example code and vignettes run correctly.
- Testthat unit tests are included and pass.

**Additional notes:**
- This package is intended for analysis of autosomal biallelic SNPs in
trios.
- Sample IDs in the VCF must match the FamilyIndex table.
- Quality control is recommended prior to running PARATI (call rate, MAF,
Mendelian inconsistencies).

Please let me know if you require any additional information.
Thank you for your consideration.

Best regards,
Jinyi Che
Email: chejinyi777 at gmail.com
GitHub: https://github.com/newche/PARATI

It enables SNP-specific analyses of genetic nurture and transgenerational
effects. The package supports homozygous trios, mixed
homozygote/heterozygote, and triple heterozygotes using haplotype
similarity with a user-defined window.

**Dependencies:**
- Imports: data.table, dplyr, stringr, optparse, openxlsx, vcfR, methods
- Suggests: knitr, rmarkdown, BiocStyle, testthat
- Optional: PLINK 1.9 (for exporting PLINK bed/bim/fam files)

**Vignette and Examples:**
- The package contains a vignette `parati-workflow.Rmd` demonstrating a
minimal example using the included testing dataset
(`Toy_TrioGenotype.vcf.gz` and `Toy_FamilyIndexTable.xlsx`).

**Testing:**
- R CMD check completed successfully without errors.
- All example code and vignettes run correctly.
- Testthat unit tests are included and pass.

**Additional notes:**
- This package is intended for analysis of autosomal biallelic SNPs in
trios.
- Sample IDs in the VCF must match the FamilyIndex table.
- Quality control is recommended prior to running PARATI (call rate, MAF,
Mendelian inconsistencies).

Please let me know if you require any additional information.
Thank you for your consideration.

Best regards,

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor package based on Julia?
messages.body_plain:
Hi Verena,

I personally don't have any experience with using Julia within an R 
package, and I don't think we have any packages in Bioconductor that do 
this at the moment. As you mentioned, the implication is that users will 
need a Julia installation for the package to run. Ask yourself how many 
users are going to be comfortable doing this. I don't know how 
mainstream Julia has become at this point, and how easy it is to 
install. Is it available on the 4 major platforms that we officially 
support, i.e. on Linux, Windows, Intel Mac, and Mac Silicon?

It also means that we will need to install Julia on our build machines. 
And also that, in the near future, the builds on r-universe will be able 
to accommodate this requirement (I'm mentioning this in the context of 
an ongoing effort by the core team to move the Bioconductor daily builds 
to the r-universe build facilities).

At the very least, you'll need to:

1. List Julia in the SystemRequirements field of the package.

2. Provide an INSTALL file that explains how to install Julia on the 4 
major platforms that we support. See 
https://contributions.bioconductor.org/sysdep.html?q=install#sysdep

They have JuliaCall and JuliaConnectoR on CRAN for interfacing R with 
Julia code, which I'm not familiar with either, but it seems to me that 
you'd want to leverage that.

Sorry for the very generic answer. Hopefully someone on this mailing 
list has some experience with Julia in R and can give more useful 
recommendations.

Best,

H.

> Many thanks and kind regards,

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor package based on Julia?
messages.body_plain:
Hi Verena,

Polyglot code can be tricky as Herv? points out. Another option, if you're open to using Rcpp in your Bioconductor package, is the mlpack C++ header-only library that interfaces with Julia, R, Python, and Go (mlpack.org) and there is a spack package that may simplify the installation for you (packages.spack.io/package.html?name=mlpack) if you're not keen on containers and/or prefer native architecture compilation.

That way, you're specifying an external dependency on mlpack only for which you can also create an R package (CRAN has plenty of C++ header-only packages you can refer to) and to Herv?'s point you could limit certain unit tests that are julia-specific to CI systems that provide that functionality (perhaps your own).

I don't use mlpack and don't know how well it would suit your purpose; you don't describe what Julia packages or functionality you're using the Julia code for. mlpack is the only relevant result that showed up from searching for polyglot r packages in the spack repository with julia [1].

As Herv? mentions, you would have to think carefully about your user setup experience and how maintainable it would be to have robust packaging with Julia code compared to rewriting the Julia code into existing R packages or setup that requires fewer additional steps and moving parts. I don't meant to discourage you from what you're doing: polyglot code is more common and in large scientific codes but can need a bit of work to maintain within language-specific package managers or require a language-agnostic package manager.

Pariksheet

[1] Namely:
git clone https://github.com/spack/spack.git spack
cd spack
source share/spack/setup-env.sh
spack repo update
spack cd -P
grep -lw r packages/*/*.py | xargs grep -F julia
less packages/mlpack/package.py
